# refs-download Specification

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Status | Active |
| Version | 1.0.0 |
| Created | 2026-02-20 |
| Depends | git, python3, scripts/refs-setup.sh, refs/refs.yaml |

## Overview

The `refs-download` skill populates external reference materials under the
`refs/` directory by delegating to `scripts/refs-setup.sh`. It provides a
user-friendly slash-command interface over the setup script, handling clone,
update, status, and clean operations for all sources defined in
`refs/refs.yaml`.

The key design decision is **sparse-by-default** for large repositories
(notably `openwifi-reference-docs`): only markdown and index files are checked out,
skipping hundreds of megabytes of origin PDFs while retaining full AI search
capability.

**Who uses it:** Developers setting up a new workspace or refreshing reference
materials for AI-assisted development.

**When:** During initial project setup, after adding new sources to
`refs/refs.yaml`, or periodically to pull upstream updates.

## Architecture

### Directory Layout

```
refs/
  refs.yaml                # Manifest: defines all reference sources
  INDEX.md                 # Master index (generated by refs-index skill)
  <source-name>/           # Cloned content (gitignored)
    .git/
    .index.md              # Per-source index (generated by refs-index skill)
    ...
scripts/
  refs-setup.sh            # Implementation script (380 lines, bash)
  refs-activate.sh         # KB skill installer (called automatically post-clone)
```

### Data Flow

```
/refs-download [args]
  -> parse mode (default/full) and target
    -> invoke scripts/refs-setup.sh with mapped flags
      -> refs-setup.sh reads refs/refs.yaml
        -> git clone/fetch/checkout per source
          -> refs-activate.sh installs KB skills
```

## Interface / Subcommands

| Invocation | Mapped Script Command | Description |
|------------|----------------------|-------------|
| `/refs-download` | `refs-setup.sh` | Clone all sources; sparse for `openwifi-reference-docs` |
| `/refs-download --full` | `refs-setup.sh --full` | Clone all with full content (including origin PDFs) |
| `/refs-download <source>` | `refs-setup.sh <source>` | Clone or update a single source |
| `/refs-download <source> --full` | `refs-setup.sh <source> --full` | Single source, full mode |
| `/refs-download update` | `refs-setup.sh update` | Pull latest for all cloned sources |
| `/refs-download update <source>` | `refs-setup.sh update <source>` | Pull latest for one source |
| `/refs-download status` | `refs-setup.sh status` | Show clone status, branches, disk usage |
| `/refs-download clean` | `refs-setup.sh clean` | Remove all cloned reference content |

### Flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--full` | Clone everything including `origin/` PDFs. Without this flag, sources with `sparse_default` in `refs.yaml` use sparse checkout. |

## Logic / Workflow

### Default Clone (no `--full`)

1. Read `refs/refs.yaml` to enumerate sources and their configuration.
2. For each source:
   - If `sparse` is set: always use sparse checkout with listed paths.
   - If `sparse_default` is set and `--full` is not specified: use sparse checkout (e.g., `md/` and `skills/` only for `openwifi-reference-docs`).
   - Otherwise: full `git clone`.
3. For branch-tracked sources (`mode: branch`), check out the specified branch.
   For pinned sources (`mode: pin`), check out the specified SHA.
4. After all clones complete, run `scripts/refs-activate.sh` to install KB
   skills from any sources containing `kb.yaml`.
5. Report completion status.

### Full Clone (`--full`)

Same as default, except `sparse_default` entries are ignored -- all content
is cloned. If a source was previously cloned in sparse mode, `refs-setup.sh`
upgrades it to full by disabling sparse checkout and running `git checkout`.

### Update

1. For each already-cloned source:
   - Branch-tracked: `git fetch origin && git pull origin <branch>`.
   - Pinned: compare current HEAD against pinned SHA; fetch and checkout if different.
2. If `--full` is specified, also upgrade any sparse checkouts to full.
3. Re-run `refs-activate.sh` to pick up any new KB skills.

### Status

Display a table with columns: NAME, STATUS (OK/DIRTY/MISSING), REF
(branch or pinned SHA), HEAD (short hash), SIZE (disk usage), CHECKOUT
(full or sparse with listed paths), DESCRIPTION.

## Safety Rules

- **Never modify `refs/refs.yaml`** from this skill. The manifest is maintained manually by developers.
- **Cloned content is gitignored.** The `refs/` directory contents (except `refs.yaml` and `INDEX.md`) are in `.gitignore`. Never attempt to commit cloned source content to the prplos repo.
- **SSH key required.** All source URIs use `git@` SSH protocol. The skill will fail clearly if SSH authentication is not configured.
- **No destructive operations without `clean`.** The `update` subcommand only fetches and fast-forwards; it never force-resets or discards local changes.
- **Sparse checkout preserves disk space.** The default mode avoids downloading large binaries. Only `--full` triggers full content retrieval.

## Dependencies

| Dependency | Type | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| `scripts/refs-setup.sh` | Script | All git operations (clone, update, status, clean) |
| `scripts/refs-activate.sh` | Script | Post-clone KB skill installation |
| `refs/refs.yaml` | Manifest | Source definitions (URI, branch/pin, sparse config) |
| `git` | CLI tool | Clone, fetch, sparse checkout |
| `python3` | Runtime | YAML parsing within `refs-setup.sh` |
| SSH keys | Auth | Access to Bitbucket/GitLab repositories |

### Disk Usage Estimates

| Source | Default (sparse) | Full |
|--------|-------------------|------|
| optimeventproto | ~5 MB | ~5 MB |
| optim-provision-model | ~120 KB | ~120 KB |
| optim5_doc | ~14 MB | ~14 MB |
| openwifi-reference-docs | ~5-20 MB | ~200+ MB |
